article_title: Gradle 9.5 Ships Task Provenance in Error Messages, Type-Safe Kotlin Settings Plugins, and Wrapper Retry Support

content_preview.title: Gradle 9.5 Ships Task Provenance in Error Messages, Type-Safe Kotlin Settings Plugins, and Wrapper Retry Support
content_preview.summary: Gradle 9.5.0 and its patch 9.5.1 bring clearer diagnostics, CI-resilient wrapper downloads, and long-awaited Kotlin DSL improvements.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Gradle 9.5.0, released on April 28, 2026, adds a trio of improvements that developers and plugin authors have been requesting for years: error messages that tell you exactly where a failing task was registered, type-safe Kotlin accessors for settings-level convention plugins, and opt-in automatic retries for wrapper distribution downloads. A patch release, 9.5.1, followed on May 12, 2026, resolving three bugs introduced in 9.5.0.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, technically detailed coverage of Gradle 9.5.0 and 9.5.1. Three headline features are explained clearly with concrete examples including code snippets. Additional improvements section provides good depth.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources verified. source-0.html.gz (Gradle 9.5.0 release notes): confirmed task provenance, retryBackOffMs, disallowChanges(), GRADLE_DAEMON_BIND_ADDRESS, --into, kotlin-dsl features. source-1.html.gz (9.5.1 release notes): confirmed three regression fixes; note the release notes say 2026-05-14 but the releases page says May 12 - the article correctly cited the releases page. source-2.html.gz (April 2026 Gradle Newsletter): verified all quotes: 'when a task fails, Gradle now tells you whether it was registered...', 'same IDE autocompletion and compile-time checking as Project-level conventions', 'reduce flaky CI failures on unstable networks', and 'More than 30 practices' for IntelliJ. source-3.html.gz (gradle.org/releases): confirmed all release dates - 9.4.0 Mar 04, 9.4.1 Mar 19, 9.5.0 Apr 28, 9.5.1 May 12.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims verified against sources. Release dates, GitHub issue numbers, and feature descriptions are accurate. All quoted text matches source verbatim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Gradle 9.5 article found. Only prior Gradle coverage is a Kotlin 2.3.2 article from April mentioning Gradle 9.3 support - no overlap.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, well-sourced article ready for publication. All sources verified and quotes confirmed verbatim.
